name: Tecton Feature Platform
what_it_does: Enterprise feature platform built by the team behind Feast and Uber's Michelangelo. Tecton manages the full feature lifecycle: batch, streaming, and real-time feature computation with automatic materialization to online and offline stores. Provides feature monitoring, lineage, and governance. REST API and Python SDK for feature retrieval. Strong focus on production reliability and data freshness SLAs.
use_cases: Serve real-time ML features to agent inference with freshness guarantees via Tecton's managed online store with sub-10ms latency
Compute streaming features (e.g., user activity in last 5 minutes) for real-time agent personalization without custom stream processing
Share feature definitions across multiple agent models to ensure training-serving consistency
Monitor feature drift and freshness via Tecton's built-in feature monitoring for agent ML model quality assurance
Build feature pipelines with automatic backfill for historical training data and continuous materialization for production
not_for: Small ML teams — Tecton is enterprise-priced with significant implementation overhead
Simple batch ML without real-time serving needs — Feast or custom pipelines are more cost-effective
Teams needing open-source, self-hosted solutions — Tecton is a managed SaaS platform
best_when: You're a mature ML organization with production real-time ML features, multiple teams sharing features, and strict freshness SLA requirements.
avoid_when: You're early-stage, have a small ML team, or don't need real-time features — the cost and complexity of Tecton isn't justified.

auth.notes: Service account API keys for production automation. OAuth for interactive developer access. Keys scoped by workspace and permission level. Tecton manages SSO integration for enterprise.

pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise pricing based on compute, storage, and online serving volume. Typically $100,000-$500,000+/year.
pricing.notes: Tecton is enterprise SaaS. No self-serve sign-up. Evaluation environments available via sales. Compute costs run on your cloud (AWS/GCP) — Tecton charges platform fees on top.

performance.rate_limits: Online feature serving rate limits based on contract; typically 10,000-100,000 QPS for enterprise

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Python SDK raises typed exceptions with context. REST API returns JSON errors with request IDs. Feature retrieval errors include feature service and join key context. Monitoring alerts provide proactive error detection.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Feature retrieval is fully idempotent — same entity keys and timestamps return consistent results. Materialization pipelines are idempotent with proper scheduling.
known_agent_gotchas: Feature freshness depends on streaming pipeline health — agents must monitor freshness SLAs and handle stale feature scenarios
Entity key type enforcement is strict — string vs int entity keys cause retrieval failures
Feature Service must be deployed before serving — schema changes require new Feature Service deployment
Online store latency SLAs depend on infrastructure region — cross-region feature serving adds latency
Tecton compute runs in your cloud account — IAM permission misconfiguration silently breaks materialization jobs
Breaking schema changes to feature views require careful migration with backward compatibility planning
SDK version must match Tecton server version — version drift causes API compatibility issues

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II. TLS enforced. OAuth 2.0 with workspace scoping. Compute runs in customer's cloud — data never leaves your environment. Strong enterprise security posture.
